chapter_context.summary: Boaz legally redeems Elimelech's land and marries Ruth; their son Obed becomes the grandfather of King David.

outline.text: The Legal Redemption at the Gate
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Legal Redemption at the Gate (4:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ruth.

outline.text: The Birth of Obed and Naomi's Joy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Birth of Obed and Naomi's Joy (4:13-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ruth.
This section focuses on The Genealogy of David (4:18-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ruth.

key_themes.title: Loyalty (Hesed)
key_themes.description: The covenantal faithfulness shown by Ruth to Naomi and Boaz to Ruth.

key_themes.title: Redemption
key_themes.description: God's providence in restoring the empty and broken through a redeemer.

devotional.question: How does Ruth's decision to stay with Naomi inspire your own commitment to those in need?
Where have you seen 'providential' meetings or events in your own life recently?

cultural_historical.title: The Kinsman-Redeemer
cultural_historical.text: A 'Goel' was a relative responsible for protecting family land and lineage, providing a beautiful picture of Christ's redemptive work.
